topic: Nogizaka46 sixth-generation member Rio Nagashima makes her first center appearance with “Inochi no Iro” during a hometown performance in Hokkaido.
facts:
VERIFIED FACTS (confirmed by Official Nogizaka46 website, JOYSOUND Music News, Billboard JAPAN, THE FIRST TIMES, Nikkan Sports, ORICON NEWS - use as-is, do not invent numbers/quotes beyond these):
- Rio Nagashima is the center of “Inochi no Iro,” a song performed by Nogizaka46’s sixth-generation members.
- Nogizaka46 first revealed and performed “Inochi no Iro” on July 4, 2026, during the first Hokkaido show of the “Midsummer National Tour 2026” at Makomanai Sekisui Heim Ice Arena.
- Nagashima is from Hokkaido, making the first performance a hometown debut.
- “Inochi no Iro” is Nagashima’s first center song.
- “Inochi no Iro” is included on Nogizaka46’s 42nd single, “Zehini Oyobazu,” scheduled for physical release on July 22, 2026.
- “Inochi no Iro” became available for streaming and download on July 5, 2026.
- “Inochi no Iro” entered Oricon’s July 5 daily digital-single chart at No. 11; the chart is based on digital download data collected from participating music services.
- Nagashima’s official English romanization is Rio Nagashima.

CONTESTED / SINGLE-SOURCE (attribute clearly, do not present as settled fact):
- Nagashima’s official blog expresses her wish to deliver the song directly to supporters; this is first-person material rather than independently verified reporting.
- The No. 11 Oricon position measures digital downloads, not total streams, physical sales, or overall popularity, so it should not be treated as a complete measure of the song’s success.
- Calling this the “main story” is a research judgment based on repeated coverage by several outlets, not an official ranking of Nogizaka46 news.

GENERAL KNOWLEDGE (you may explain these established concepts from your own knowledge):
- In Japanese idol groups, the “center” is the member placed at the focal position of a song’s performance and promotional presentation.
- A first center assignment is an important career milestone because it gives a member unusually high visibility within the group.
- A hometown performance can carry extra emotional weight because family, local fans, and people from the member’s home region may be watching.
- “Inochi no Iro” functions as both a sixth-generation showcase and part of Nogizaka46’s larger 42nd-single campaign.

SPECULATION (encouraged, but always clearly framed, e.g. "If X happens..."):
- If the song gains sustained attention after its hometown premiere and advance digital release, Nagashima’s center role could help establish her as a prominent representative of Nogizaka46’s sixth generation.
headlines: Official Nogizaka46 website: The 42nd single includes the sixth-generation song “Inochi no Iro,” with an advance digital release on July 5.
JOYSOUND Music News: Nogizaka46’s sixth-generation members release “Inochi no Iro,” centered by Rio Nagashima.
Billboard JAPAN: Nogizaka46 prepares an advance release of the sixth-generation song led by Rio Nagashima.
THE FIRST TIMES: The new sixth-generation song receives an early release, with Nagashima taking the center position.
Nikkan Sports, carried by Livedoor News: Nagashima takes her first center role and premieres the song at Nogizaka46’s Hokkaido concert.
ORICON NEWS: “Inochi no Iro” enters the July 5 daily digital-single chart at No. 11.
article_summaries:
Lead sentences by outlet (paraphrased close to the original opening line, not verbatim):

Official Nogizaka46 website: The announcement says the new sixth-generation song will appear on the July 22 single, with Nagashima as center and digital distribution beginning July 5.

JOYSOUND Music News: The lead introduces “Inochi no Iro” as a sixth-generation release arriving online the next day, identifies Nagashima as its center, and notes its Hokkaido concert premiere.

Billboard JAPAN: The opening summarizes the July 5 advance release and explains that the track is Nagashima’s first center song, first performed in her home region of Hokkaido.

THE FIRST TIMES: The lead reports the advance distribution announcement and frames the song around Nagashima’s first center appearance at the Hokkaido stop of the national tour.

Nikkan Sports, carried by Livedoor News: The opening focuses on Nagashima’s new center role, then explains that she introduced the song at the Hokkaido concert in her home region.

ORICON NEWS: The opening identifies the July 5 daily digital-single ranking, where “Inochi no Iro” appears as a new No. 11 entry.
